refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is refined to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the evaluation period, clarifying the requirement for measurable verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Monitor and log teleoperation link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period; calculate availability and compare against the customer-defined target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or mitigate link failures to maintain availability.
FR4: Provide accurate availability metrics to stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Use robust telemetry protocols with error correction and retransmission.
DP3: Integrate real-time link health monitoring and alerting.
DP4: Employ adaptive bandwidth management to prioritize teleoperation traffic.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

potential_sources: Interaction with Safety Services for failover alerts
coupling_check.notes: Redundancy and failover logic are localized to network management, minimizing cross-module coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of redundant network hardware and infrastructure.
Risk of increased system complexity leading to potential integration issues.
Risk that failover timing may not meet strict availability targets.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding DP3 (real-time monitoring) as a complementary DP to improve detection and response times.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for managing teleoperation link hardware and software, including redundancy and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends link status and health data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections managed within Teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts on link failures or degraded availability to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but not direct control of link redundancy.
Vehicle hardware interfaces with network but does not manage link availability.
Primary module responsible for link availability management and failover.
Receives alerts but does not manage link redundancy.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Teleoperation module encapsulates all logic and hardware control for teleoperation link availability, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Safety Services for alerting.
Risk of incomplete failover coverage if vehicle hardware changes are needed.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review interface definitions with Safety Services to ensure alerting is timely and reliable.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR of maintaining link availability.

diffusion_risks: Alerting interfaces to Safety Services may cause diffusion if not tightly controlled.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment of DP with minimal diffusion to other modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring link availability metrics and alert logs within Teleoperation and Safety Services modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of link availability functionality within Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Add complementary DP for real-time monitoring to improve detection and mitigation.
Clarify SafetyAlert interface timing and reliability requirements.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, adding real-time monitoring DP and refining alert interfaces will strengthen the design and reduce risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or mitigate link failures to maintain availability.
FR4: Provide accurate availability metrics to stakeholders.

revise.why: To incorporate complementary design parameters for monitoring and alerting that improve link availability and reduce risk.

assumptions: Redundant network hardware is available and supported.
Teleoperation module can interface reliably with Safety Services.
open_risks: Failover timing may not meet strict availability targets.
Alerting latency and reliability to Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Test data on failover performance and link availability.
Verification of alert delivery and response times.
